content_markdown:
A string is made to vibrate at its third harmonic. The diagram shows two points P and Q at a particular instant in time.

Which of the following compares the period of vibration of P and Q and the average speed of P and Q?

diagram_metadata.description: A standing wave on a string showing three loops (third harmonic). Point P is located near the peak of the first loop, and point Q is located at the trough of the second loop.

vibe_explanation: In a standing wave, all points on the string (except nodes) vibrate with the same frequency and therefore the same period. However, the amplitude of vibration varies depending on the position along the string. Since average speed over a cycle is proportional to amplitude ($v_{avg} = \frac{4A}{T}$), and P and Q are at different distances from their respective nodes/antinodes (Q is at an antinode while P is not), their average speeds are different.
